title: Salty Velvety Midnight Canadian Fish Stir-fry
description: A gluten-free friendly salty and velvety fish dish that satisfies a midnight craving for Canadian flavors. Features fish that has been blanched to perfection.
instructions: Preheat your oven to 400°F (205°C). Blanch the fish fillets in boiling water for about 2-3 minutes. Drain and set aside.
Slice the garlic thinly, scallions into thin slices, and cut the red bell pepper into strips.
Heat olive oil or butter in a wok or large skillet over medium heat. Add sliced garlic, sauté until golden brown (about 1 minute).
Add fish fillets to the pan, sprinkle with salt, and cook for about 2-3 minutes on each side, or until lightly golden.
Stir-fry water chestnuts, snow peas, and red bell pepper strips in the remaining oil. Season with a pinch of salt and black pepper (if desired).
Combine blanched fish with stir-fried vegetables. Garnish with scallion slices before serving.
